root_text.sn56.30:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ sambahulā therā bhikkhū cetesu viharanti sahañcanike.
root_text.sn56.30:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulānaṁ therānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ pacchābhattaṁ piṇḍapātapaṭikkantānaṁ maṇḍalamāḷe sannisinnānaṁ sannipatitānaṁ ayamantarākathā udapādi:
root_text.sn56.30:1.3: “yo nu kho, āvuso, dukkhaṁ passati dukkhasamudayampi so passati, dukkhanirodhampi passati, dukkhanirodhagāminiṁ paṭipadampi passatī”ti.
root_text.sn56.30:2.1: Evaṁ vutte, āyasmā gavampati thero bhikkhū etadavoca:
root_text.sn56.30:2.2: “sammukhā metaṁ, āvuso, bhagavato sutaṁ, sammukhā paṭiggahitaṁ:
root_text.sn56.30:2.3: ‘yo, bhikkhave, dukkhaṁ passati dukkhasamudayampi so passati, dukkhanirodhampi passati, dukkhanirodhagāminiṁ paṭipadampi passati.
root_text.sn56.30:2.4: Yo dukkhasamudayaṁ passati dukkhampi so passati, dukkhanirodhampi passati, dukkhanirodhagāminiṁ paṭipadampi passati.
root_text.sn56.30:2.5: Yo dukkhanirodhaṁ passati dukkhampi so passati, dukkhasamudayampi passati, dukkhanirodhagāminiṁ paṭipadampi passati.
root_text.sn56.30:2.6: Yo dukkhanirodhagāminiṁ paṭipadaṁ passati dukkhampi so passati, dukkhasamudayampi passati, dukkhanirodhampi passatī’”ti.

translation_text.sn56.30:1.1: At one time several mendicants were staying in the land of the Cetīs at Sahajāti.
translation_text.sn56.30:1.2: Now at that time, after the meal, on return from almsround, several senior mendicants sat together in the pavilion and this discussion came up among them:
translation_text.sn56.30:1.3: “Reverends, does someone who sees suffering also see the origin of suffering, the cessation of suffering, and the practice that leads to the cessation of suffering?”
translation_text.sn56.30:2.1: When they said this, Venerable Gavampati said to those senior mendicants:
translation_text.sn56.30:2.2: “Reverends, I have heard and learned this in the presence of the Buddha:
translation_text.sn56.30:2.3: ‘Someone who sees suffering also sees the origin of suffering, the cessation of suffering, and the practice that leads to the cessation of suffering.
translation_text.sn56.30:2.4: Someone who sees the origin of suffering also sees suffering, the cessation of suffering, and the practice that leads to the cessation of suffering.
translation_text.sn56.30:2.5: Someone who sees the cessation of suffering also sees suffering, the origin of suffering, and the practice that leads to the cessation of suffering.
translation_text.sn56.30:2.6: Someone who sees the practice that leads to the cessation of suffering also sees suffering, the origin of suffering, and the cessation of suffering.’”

variant_text.sn56.30:1.1: cetesu → cetīsu (bj); cetiyesu (sya-all) | sahañcanike → sahajātiyaṁ (bj); sahajaniye (sya-all, km)
variant_text.sn56.30:2.1: gavampati thero → gavampatitthero (sya-all, km); gavampati there (pts1ed)
